quick_facts.label: Credential and standard duration
quick_facts.value: B.Pharm is a four-year pharmacy degree after 10+2 in the Pharmacy Council of India (PCI) framework.

quick_facts.label: Professional-registration relevance
quick_facts.value: For pharmacist registration, the B.Pharm qualification must be from an institution/course approved by PCI under section 12 of the Pharmacy Act, 1948; registration itself is administered by the relevant State Pharmacy Council, not PCI.

quick_facts.label: Typical school-subject preparation
quick_facts.value: Physics, Chemistry and Biology or Mathematics are a common prerequisite pattern. For example, BITS Pilani's 2026 B.Pharm admission eligibility requires Class 12 Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Mathematics, plus a language and one additional subject.

quick_facts.label: Admission routes vary by institution and state
quick_facts.value: B.Pharm admission is not governed by one all-India entrance route: BITS Pilani uses BITSAT for its B.Pharm intake, while WBJEE-2026 is an entrance examination for undergraduate pharmacy admissions in participating West Bengal institutions.

quick_facts.label: Fees are variable
quick_facts.value: PCI does not regulate student admission fees; fee setting is handled by the concerned Central or State Government. As a dated high-fee example, BITS Pilani listed ₹2,91,500 semester tuition for its first-degree programmes, including B.Pharm, for academic year 2026–27, before applicable hostel, mess and other charges.

sections.heading: Eligibility: build from Class 12 science, then check the target institute’s rules
paragraphs: PCI identifies the Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm) Course Regulations, 2014 as the governing regulation for B.Pharm admission qualifications. Applicants should treat the chosen college’s current prospectus and counselling rules as decisive, because entrance tests, marks thresholds, domicile rules and reservation requirements differ by institution and state.

A representative 2026 institutional rule is BITS Pilani’s: B.Pharm applicants need Class 12 under the 10+2 system with Physics, Chemistry and Biology or Mathematics, a language and an additional subject. BITS requires at least 75% aggregate in Physics, Chemistry and Biology/Mathematics and 60% in each of those subjects; applicants entering through PCB must have Class 10 mathematics proficiency. These are BITS-specific requirements, not a universal national cutoff.
bullets: Before applying, confirm that the programme is B.Pharm and that the institution has the PCI approval status needed for registration as a pharmacist.
Check the exact academic-year eligibility and counselling bulletin for every target college; do not assume that qualifying one entrance examination guarantees eligibility everywhere.

sections.heading: Duration and regulatory status
paragraphs: B.Pharm is structured as a four-year course after 10+2. It is one of the pharmacy qualifications PCI lists as approved for the purpose of pharmacist registration when completed at an institution approved under section 12 of the Pharmacy Act, 1948.

This approval distinction matters. PCI states that a B.Pharm course may be conducted only in institutions approved by it under section 12 for this purpose. A prospective student should verify the particular college and academic-session approval rather than relying only on advertising, university affiliation or the course title.
bullets: Study length: four years after Class 12.
Licensure pathway: apply to the State Pharmacy Council after completing an eligible, PCI-approved qualification and meeting the applicable statutory conditions.
State Pharmacy Councils handle registration, renewal and transfer of pharmacist registration.

sections.heading: Curriculum: PCI-prescribed syllabus delivered through the university or examining authority
paragraphs: The PCI approval scheme states that the syllabus for each B.Pharm subject is prescribed by PCI from time to time under regulation 8 of the Bachelor of Pharmacy (B.Pharm) Course Regulations, 2014. The detailed semester sequence, examination scheme, academic calendar and local implementation are set through the relevant university or examining authority within that regulatory framework.

When comparing colleges, ask for the current scheme of study, laboratory access, practical training arrangements, examination regulations and the exact PCI approval status for the intake year. These checks are more useful than assuming all programmes with a similar title provide the same delivery conditions.
bullets: Use the current university syllabus—not an old coaching syllabus or brochure—as the working academic reference.
Confirm PCI approval and university affiliation before paying fees or accepting a seat.

sections.heading: Admission: use the route required by the target college
paragraphs: Admissions are decentralised. For 2026–27, BITS Pilani offered B.Pharm admissions through BITSAT, while the West Bengal Joint Entrance Examinations Board conducted WBJEE-2026 for undergraduate pharmacy admissions in participating universities, government colleges and self-financing institutes in West Bengal. Other institutions and state counselling systems can use different entrance examinations, merit rules or processes.

Application windows and counselling dates are time-sensitive. For context, WBJEE-2026 registration ran from March 10 to April 8, 2026, and its examination was held on May 24, 2026. Those dates are historical as of July 19, 2026 and should not be reused for a later cycle.
bullets: Make a target-college list first, then identify the applicable examination or counselling authority for each institution.
Read the current information bulletin for eligibility, seat categories, document verification, choice filling and fee-refund rules.
For BITS, BITSAT is the stated admission test for B.Pharm; BITS also publishes separate eligibility and fee documents for its own cycle.

sections.heading: Costs: compare official fee schedules rather than relying on national averages
paragraphs: There is no single PCI-set B.Pharm tuition fee: PCI explicitly says that institutional admission fees are not regulated by PCI and are handled by the concerned Central or State Government. Fees therefore vary substantially by state, institution type, quota, residence/hostel status and annual revision policy.

As a dated example only, BITS Pilani’s 2026–27 first-degree fee schedule—covering B.Pharm—listed ₹64,100 admission fee and ₹2,91,500 semester tuition for the first and second semesters, plus separate annual, hostel, mess/electricity and other charges where applicable. BITS states that tuition and hostel fees are revised upward annually for that cohort. This is not a benchmark for government, state-counselling or other private colleges.
bullets: Request the official current-year fee circular and identify tuition, one-time admission charges, hostel, mess, examination, transport and refundable deposits separately.
Ask whether quoted fees cover all four years or only the first academic year, and whether scholarships or tuition-fee-waiver seats have separate eligibility rules.

sections.heading: What B.Pharm can lead to
paragraphs: A PCI-approved B.Pharm can support the pharmacist-registration route. The graduate applies to the appropriate State Pharmacy Council; PCI notes that State Pharmacy Councils, rather than PCI, carry out registration, renewal and transfers. The statutory framework also requires the applicant to meet applicable age, residence/business-or-profession-in-the-state, fee and approved-qualification conditions.

For postgraduate study, PCI lists M.Pharm as a two-year course after B.Pharm. NIPERs offer postgraduate M.Pharm, M.S. (Pharm.), M.Tech. (Pharm.) and MBA (Pharm.) programmes, with admissions through the NIPER JEE process. The 2026 NIPER JEE cycle covered master's programmes, so candidates should consult the current brochure and programme-specific eligibility rather than assume every B.Pharm degree fits every specialization.

B.Pharm is also relevant to drug-regulatory career paths, but it does not by itself guarantee appointment as a Drug Inspector. The Drugs Rules, 1945 include pharmacy/pharmaceutical-chemistry degree qualifications for specified inspector appointments, while the exact recruiting authority, experience conditions, examination and vacancies are determined by the applicable recruitment notification. Graduates may also pursue industry, sales and entrepreneurship roles, or use their bachelor's degree for broader examinations such as management and civil-services pathways, subject to the separate eligibility rules of those examinations.
bullets: Pharmacist: complete an eligible PCI-approved B.Pharm and apply through the relevant State Pharmacy Council.
M.Pharm and research-oriented postgraduate routes: consider GPAT where required or useful, and NIPER JEE for eligible NIPER master's programmes.
Drug Inspector: monitor official central or state recruitment notices and verify their current qualification and experience requirements.
Management, civil services and entrepreneurship: these are separate pathways; their selection rules are not part of B.Pharm licensure.
